title: A letter to a friend who moved away
summary: Write a short, friendly letter to a friend who moved to a different city, using present simple to describe your current life.
topic: Writing to a friend who moved to a different city
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences as an informal letter to a friend who recently moved away. Tell them what is new in your life and ask them two questions about their new city. Use present simple. Start with 'Dear [name],' and end with a closing line.
hintStarters: Dear Amara, I hope you are…

hintVocab.meaningVi: quán cà phê cũ của chúng ta
giữ gìn sức khỏe / bảo trọng

sampleText:
Dear Amara,

I hope you are well. It is strange here without you. I still go to our old café on Friday mornings but it feels different now because you are not there. I usually sit at our table near the window. The coffee is still good but I always drink it alone.

Things are a little different at work too. We have a new manager and he is quite strict. I sometimes stay late on Tuesdays because there is a lot to do. I never leave the office before seven o'clock on those days.

I have two questions for you. Is your new neighborhood busy or quiet? And do you have a good coffee shop near your flat? I hope you find a nice place to sit and read.

I miss you a lot. Please write back soon and tell me everything about your new city.

Take care,
Priya

sampleAnnotations.phrase: It is strange here without you
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Nơi này thật lạ lẫm khi không có bạn
quán cà phê cũ của chúng ta
bây giờ nó cảm thấy khác
bàn của chúng ta gần cửa sổ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Is your new neighborhood busy or quiet
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Khu phố mới của bạn có đông đúc hay yên tĩnh không

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for current facts and habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để nói về những điều đúng ngay bây giờ hoặc những thói quen hiện tại: 'I still go to our old café', 'I usually sit at our table', 'I sometimes stay late'. Các phó từ tần suất đặt trước động từ chính.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for facts that are true now or for current habits: 'I still go', 'I usually sit', 'I sometimes stay late'. Adverbs of frequency go before the main verb.

sampleTranslationVi:
Amara thân mến,

Tôi hy vọng bạn khỏe. Nơi này thật lạ lẫm khi không có bạn. Tôi vẫn đến quán cà phê cũ của chúng ta vào sáng thứ Sáu nhưng bây giờ nó cảm thấy khác vì bạn không ở đó. Tôi thường ngồi ở bàn của chúng ta gần cửa sổ. Cà phê vẫn ngon nhưng tôi luôn uống một mình.

Mọi thứ ở chỗ làm cũng hơi khác. Chúng tôi có một quản lý mới và anh ấy khá nghiêm khắc. Đôi khi tôi ở lại muộn vào thứ Ba vì có nhiều việc cần làm. Những ngày đó tôi không bao giờ rời văn phòng trước bảy giờ.

Tôi có hai câu hỏi cho bạn. Khu phố mới của bạn đông đúc hay yên tĩnh? Và gần căn hộ của bạn có quán cà phê ngon không? Tôi hy vọng bạn tìm được chỗ ngồi đọc sách thật dễ chịu.

Tôi nhớ bạn rất nhiều. Hãy viết thư lại sớm và kể cho tôi nghe mọi thứ về thành phố mới của bạn.

Bảo trọng nhé,
Priya

mcQuestions.prompt: Which is the correct way to open an informal letter to a friend?
options: Dear Amara,
To Whom It May Concern,
Greetings Amara!
Hello my dear friend Amara I hope you are well and fine.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Dear [name],' is the standard informal letter opening in English.
mcQuestions.hint: Which opening is both friendly and correctly punctuated?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present simple to describe a current habit?
options: I was still going to our old café on Friday mornings.
I still go to our old café on Friday mornings.
I will still go to our old café on Friday mornings.
I am still going to our old café right now.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple with 'still' and 'on Friday mornings' shows a regular current habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a present tense verb with a time phrase showing repetition.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' correctly to give a reason?
options: It feels different because not you there.
Because you not there it different feels.
It feels different because you are not there.
It feels because different you not there.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' + subject + verb + place gives the reason correctly.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a full clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly asks a question using 'do'?
options: Have you a good coffee shop near your flat?
You have a good coffee shop near your flat?
Do you has a good coffee shop near flat?
Do you have a good coffee shop near your flat?
mcQuestions.explanation: Yes/no questions with 'have' use 'Do you have…?' in the present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Which auxiliary do you need for a present simple yes/no question with 'I/you/we/they'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which is a correct informal closing for a letter to a friend?
options: Take care,
Yours faithfully,
Yours sincerely,
With professional regards,
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Take care,' is a natural, warm informal closing. 'Yours faithfully' and 'sincerely' are formal.
mcQuestions.hint: Which closing sounds like something you say to a friend, not a boss?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show a contrast in a letter?
options: The coffee is good but drinking alone always.
The coffee is good but I always drink it alone.
The coffee good but I alone always drink.
But the coffee is good I alone drink always.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'good but I always drink it alone' — two complete clauses joined by 'but'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both sides of 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'sometimes' correctly in a letter?
options: I stay sometimes late on Tuesdays.
Sometimes I stays late on Tuesdays.
I sometimes stay late on Tuesdays.
I stay late on Tuesdays sometimes always.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'sometimes' before the main verb is standard; with 'I', the verb takes no -s.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'sometimes' sit and what ending does the verb take with 'I'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which question is correctly formed to ask about a new neighbourhood?
options: Your new neighborhood is busy or quiet?
Is busy or quiet your new neighborhood?
Does your new neighborhood is busy or quiet?
Is your new neighborhood busy or quiet?
mcQuestions.explanation: Questions with 'be': invert subject and verb: 'Is your new neighborhood…?'
mcQuestions.hint: How do you form a question with the verb 'be'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence tells a friend what you want them to do?
options: Please write back soon.
Please to write back soon.
You please write back soon.
Write please back soon you.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Please + base verb' is the standard polite imperative in English.
mcQuestions.hint: What verb form follows 'please' in a polite request?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best expresses that you miss someone?
options: I am missing you a lot all the time very much.
I miss you a lot.
I miss so much you a lot.
You I miss very lot much.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I miss you a lot' is natural and concise — object immediately after verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep the word order: subject + verb + object + adverb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If a close friend moved far away before phones and the internet existed, how do you think people kept their friendships alive? What would you write in your first letter?
